performanceFindings.description: O(n^2) pairwise loop replaced with inverted-index candidate pruning, reducing to O(E+C) for sparse graphs

principleNotes: Simplicity First: single new function, minimal code delta
Surgical Changes: only the loop replaced, all surrounding logic untouched
Goal-Driven Execution: 13/13 tests green as verification

evidence: All 4 constraints satisfied (stdlib only, tests pass, ordering preserved, COUPLED_WITH identical)
Changed-scope discipline: only coupling.py and test_context_coupling.py modified
API surface (compute_coupling signature, CouplingResult dataclass) unchanged
13/13 tests pass (12 original + 1 new scaling test)
_build_candidate_pairs correctly builds inverted index and generates canonical pairs
Set deduplication handles overlapping posting lists
name_map lookup safe: candidates from neighbor_sets.keys() subset of node_ids
Worst-case O(n^2) only when all symbols share same neighbor (unavoidable, correct)
